behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions (month 13, day 32, malformed syntax) with descriptive errors.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: parse-string validation and error-message content for invalid cron fields/syntax. Hypothetical elsewhere: parser integration with scheduler execution, persistence, or runtime clocks. MOCK-MISS: no explicit cross-module handoff is required by this behavior; it targets parser logic itself. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: mocking is unnecessary; failure is in pure parsing/validation. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: higher levels were rejected because behavior text is only about parser rejection + message quality, not runtime/infrastructure interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Call the parser function directly with invalid cron strings and assert it returns/throws structured errors containing field-specific descriptive messages (e.g., invalid month/day, malformed token position).

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard expressions, including last-day-of-month and nearest-weekday edge cases.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: correctness of computed future timestamps from cron expressions, including special cron semantics ('last day', 'nearest weekday'). Hypothetical elsewhere: external job runner, distributed clocks, networked schedulers. MOCK-MISS: real failure can occur at parser-to-schedule-calculator boundary for special tokens, not only inside one pure function. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing parser or schedule objects could hide token-encoding mismatches, so wire real parser + calculator. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Integration brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess dependency in behavior text, (b) no AI/ML output dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process wiring of cron parser and next-run calculator: provide cron strings plus a fixed start time, compute next N timestamps, and assert exact expected sequences for normal and edge expressions ('L', nearest weekday semantics).

behaviors.description: Overlap detector flags schedules firing within a minimum gap and reports conflicting windows.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: two schedules, run-time generation, minimum-gap comparison, and reported conflict windows. Hypothetical elsewhere: real queue contention, host-level timing jitter. MOCK-MISS: bug can appear when schedule-expansion output is consumed by overlap logic (module boundary), so single-function unit may miss it. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required by behavior text. MOCK-HIDE: mocking run times could hide boundary-condition defects in real schedule expansion inputs. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Integration brake: (a) no required real infrastructure, (b) no AI/ML dependency, (c) no multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser/schedule-expansion with overlap detector in-process; generate runs for two cron expressions and a 5-minute minimum gap, then assert detected conflicts and exact reported time windows at boundary cases (just-inside vs just-outside gap).

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling handles DST spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity for a 2:30 AM job.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: timezone-aware timestamp computation across DST transition rules, including nonexistent and ambiguous local times. Hypothetical elsewhere: OS clock skew, NTP drift, cross-host timezone config drift. MOCK-MISS: failure often appears where scheduler logic interacts with timezone-rule library/data; mocking timezone behavior can mask real DST rule handling defects. REAL-INFRA: behavior does not require real network/subprocess/OS events; in-process timezone library with real tz rules is sufficient. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate for System: behavior text focuses on computed run times across DST semantics, not OS-level time APIs or external services.
behaviors.test_description: Run parser + timezone-aware scheduler with real timezone rules (e.g., IANA zone) over concrete DST transition dates; assert handling policy for nonexistent 2:30 during spring-forward and deterministic handling of duplicated 2:30 during fall-back.

behaviors.description: If next run time already passed, engine applies missed-run policy (skip vs backfill).
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: interaction between computed next-run time, current time, and policy-driven decision (skip/backfill). Hypothetical elsewhere: persisted outage recovery from external DB or distributed replay systems. MOCK-MISS: defects can occur at handoff between schedule computation and policy evaluator; pure unit of one function may miss configuration/state wiring issues. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required by behavior text. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing either schedule output or policy config can hide incorrect integration of decision path. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Integration brake: (a) no required real infrastructure, (b) no AI/ML dependency, (c) no multi-agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: In-process integration test across calculator + missed-run policy module: set start/now so next run is in the past, execute decision logic for each policy, and assert resulting action (skip or generated backfill run set) and resulting schedule cursor.
